NKJV.1: “Brethren  and fathers, hear my defense before you now.”
NKJV.2: And when they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they kept all the more silent. Then he said:
NKJV.3: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, taught according to the strictness of our fathers’ law, and was zealous toward God as you all are today.
NKJV.4: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women,
NKJV.5: as also the high priest bears me witness, and all the council of the elders, from whom I also received letters to the brethren, and went to Damascus to bring in chains even those who were there to Jerusalem to be punished.
NKJV.6: “Now it happened, as I journeyed and came near Damascus at about noon, suddenly a great light from heaven shone around me.
NKJV.7: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?’
NKJV.8: So I answered, ‘Who are You, Lord?’ And He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.’
NKJV.9: “And those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they did not hear the voice of Him who spoke to me.
NKJV.10: So I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ And the Lord said to me, ‘Arise and go into Damascus, and there you will be told all things which are appointed for you to do.’
NKJV.11: And since I could not see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.
NKJV.12: “Then a certain Ananias, a devout man according to the law, having a good testimony with all the Jews who dwelt there,
NKJV.13: came to me; and he stood and said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight.’ And at that same hour I looked up at him.
NKJV.14: Then he said, ‘The God of our fathers has chosen you that you should know His will, and see the Just One, and hear the voice of His mouth.
NKJV.15: For you will be His witness to all men of what you have seen and heard.
NKJV.16: And now why are you waiting? Arise and be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.’
NKJV.17: “Now it happened, when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I was in a trance
NKJV.18: and saw Him saying to me, ‘Make haste and get out of Jerusalem quickly, for they will not receive your testimony concerning Me.’
NKJV.19: So I said, ‘Lord, they know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believe on You.
NKJV.20: And when the blood of Your martyr Stephen was shed, I also was standing by consenting to his death, and guarding the clothes of those who were killing him.’
NKJV.21: Then He said to me, ‘Depart,  for I will send you far from here to the Gentiles.’ ”
NKJV.22: And they listened to him until this word, and then they raised their voices and said, “Away with such a fellow from the earth, for he is not fit to live!”
NKJV.23: Then, as they cried out and tore off their clothes and threw dust into the air,
NKJV.24: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, and said that he should be examined under scourging, so that he might know why they shouted so against him.
NKJV.25: And as they bound him with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who stood by, “Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and uncondemned?”
NKJV.26: When the centurion heard that, he went and told the commander, saying, “Take care what you do, for this man is a Roman.”
NKJV.27: Then the commander came and said to him, “Tell me, are you a Roman?” He said, “Yes.”
NKJV.28: The commander answered, “With a large sum I obtained this citizenship.” And Paul said, “But I was born a citizen.”
NKJV.29: Then immediately those who were about to examine him withdrew from him; and the commander was also afraid after he found out that he was a Roman, and because he had bound him.
NKJV.30: The next day, because he wanted to know for certain why he was accused by the Jews, he released him from his bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all their council to appear, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

ESV.1: “Brothers and fathers, hear the defense that I now make before you.”
ESV.2: And when they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew language,  they became even more quiet. And he said:
ESV.3: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated at the feet of Gamaliel  according to the strict manner of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God as all of you are this day.
ESV.4: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering to prison both men and women,
ESV.5: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can bear me witness. From them I received letters to the brothers, and I journeyed toward Damascus to take those also who were there and bring them in bonds to Jerusalem to be punished.
ESV.6: “As I was on my way and drew near to Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone around me.
ESV.7: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
ESV.8: And I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ And he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting.’
ESV.9: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not understand  the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
ESV.10: And I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ And the Lord said to me, ‘Rise, and go into Damascus, and there you will be told all that is appointed for you to do.’
ESV.11: And since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me, and came into Damascus.
ESV.12: “And one Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
ESV.13: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight.’ And at that very hour I received my sight and saw him.
ESV.14: And he said, ‘The God of our fathers appointed you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear a voice from his mouth;
ESV.15: for you will be a witness for him to everyone of what you have seen and heard.
ESV.16: And now why do you wait? Rise and be baptized and wash away your sins, calling on his name.’
ESV.17: “When I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
ESV.18: and saw him saying to me,  ‘Make haste and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
ESV.19: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in one synagogue after another I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
ESV.20: And when the blood of Stephen your witness was being shed, I myself was standing by and approving and watching over the garments of those who killed him.’
ESV.21: And he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
ESV.22: Up to this word they listened to him. Then they raised their voices and said, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
ESV.23: And as they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
ESV.24: the tribune ordered him to be brought into the barracks, saying that he should be examined by flogging, to find out why they were shouting against him like this.
ESV.25: But when they had stretched him out for the whips,  Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it lawful for you to flog a man who is a Roman citizen and uncondemned?”
ESV.26: When the centurion heard this, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman citizen.”
ESV.27: So the tribune came and said to him, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
ESV.28: The tribune answered, “I bought this citizenship for a large sum.” Paul said, “But I am a citizen by birth.”
ESV.29: So those who were about to examine him withdrew from him immediately, and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
ESV.30: But on the next day, desiring to know the real reason why he was being accused by the Jews, he unbound him and commanded the chief priests and all the council to meet, and he brought Paul down and set him before them.

NASB.1: 'Brethren and fathers, hear my defense which I now [offer] to you.'
NASB.2: And when they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew dialect, they became even more quiet; and he said,
NASB.3: 'I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated under Gamaliel, strictly according to the law of our fathers, being zealous for God just as you all are today.
NASB.4: 'I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and putting both men and women into prisons,
NASB.5: as also the high priest and all the Council of the elders can testify. From them I also received letters to the brethren, and started off for Damascus in order to bring even those who were there to Jerusalem as prisoners to be punished.
NASB.6: 'But it happened that as I was on my way, approaching Damascus about noontime, a very bright light suddenly flashed from heaven all around me,
NASB.7: and I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?'
NASB.8: 'And I answered, 'Who are You, Lord?' And He said to me, 'I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are persecuting.'
NASB.9: 'And those who were with me saw the light, to be sure, but did not understand the voice of the One who was speaking to me.
NASB.10: 'And I said, 'What shall I do, Lord?' And the Lord said to me, 'Get up and go on into Damascus, and there you will be told of all that has been appointed for you to do.'
NASB.11: 'But since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me and came into Damascus.
NASB.12: 'A certain Ananias, a man who was devout by the standard of the Law, [and] well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
NASB.13: came to me, and standing near said to me, 'Brother Saul, receive your sight!' And at that very time I looked up at him.
NASB.14: 'And he said, 'The God of our fathers has appointed you to know His will and to see the Righteous One and to hear an utterance from His mouth.
NASB.15: 'For you will be a witness for Him to all men of what you have seen and heard.
NASB.16: 'Now why do you delay? Get up and be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on His name.'
NASB.17: 'It happened when I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, that I fell into a trance,
NASB.18: and I saw Him saying to me, 'Make haste, and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about Me.'
NASB.19: 'And I said, 'Lord, they themselves understand that in one synagogue after another I used to imprison and beat those who believed in You.
NASB.20: 'And when the blood of Your witness Stephen was being shed, I also was standing by approving, and watching out for the coats of those who were slaying him.'
NASB.21: 'And He said to me, 'Go! For I will send you far away to the Gentiles.''
NASB.22: They listened to him up to this statement, and [then] they raised their voices and said, 'Away with such a fellow from the earth, for he should not be allowed to live!'
NASB.23: And as they were crying out and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust into the air,
NASB.24: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, stating that he should be examined by scourging so that he might find out the reason why they were shouting against him that way.
NASB.25: But when they stretched him out with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, 'Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman and uncondemned?'
NASB.26: When the centurion heard [this], he went to the commander and told him, saying, 'What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman.'
NASB.27: The commander came and said to him, 'Tell me, are you a Roman?' And he said, 'Yes.'
NASB.28: The commander answered, 'I acquired this citizenship with a large sum of money.' And Paul said, 'But I was actually born [a citizen].'
NASB.29: Therefore those who were about to examine him immediately let go of him; and the commander also was afraid when he found out that he was a Roman, and because he had put him in chains.
NASB.30: But on the next day, wishing to know for certain why he had been accused by the Jews, he released him and ordered the chief priests and all the Council to assemble, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

NLT.1: “Brothers and esteemed fathers,” Paul said, “listen to me as I offer my defense.”
NLT.2: When they heard him speaking in their own language, the silence was even greater.
NLT.3: Then Paul said, “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus, a city in Cilicia, and I was brought up and educated here in Jerusalem under Gamaliel. As his student, I was carefully trained in our Jewish laws and customs. I became very zealous to honor God in everything I did, just like all of you today.
NLT.4: And I persecuted the followers of the Way, hounding some to death, arresting both men and women and throwing them in prison.
NLT.5: The high priest and the whole council of elders can testify that this is so. For I received letters from them to our Jewish brothers in Damascus, authorizing me to bring the followers of the Way from there to Jerusalem, in chains, to be punished.
NLT.6: “As I was on the road, approaching Damascus about noon, a very bright light from heaven suddenly shone down around me.
NLT.7: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
NLT.8: “‘Who are you, lord?’ I asked. “And the voice replied, ‘I am Jesus the Nazarene, the one you are persecuting.’
NLT.9: The people with me saw the light but didn’t understand the voice speaking to me.
NLT.10: “I asked, ‘What should I do, Lord?’ “And the Lord told me, ‘Get up and go into Damascus, and there you will be told everything you are to do.’
NLT.11: “I was blinded by the intense light and had to be led by the hand to Damascus by my companions.
NLT.12: A man named Ananias lived there. He was a godly man, deeply devoted to the law, and well regarded by all the Jews of Damascus.
NLT.13: He came and stood beside me and said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight.’ And that very moment I could see him!
NLT.14: “Then he told me, ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will and to see the Righteous One and hear him speak.
NLT.15: For you are to be his witness, telling everyone what you have seen and heard.
NLT.16: What are you waiting for? Get up and be baptized. Have your sins washed away by calling on the name of the Lord.’
NLT.17: “After I returned to Jerusalem, I was praying in the Temple and fell into a trance.
NLT.18: I saw a vision of Jesus saying to me, ‘Hurry! Leave Jerusalem, for the people here won’t accept your testimony about me.’
NLT.19: “‘But Lord,’ I argued, ‘they certainly know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
NLT.20: And I was in complete agreement when your witness Stephen was killed. I stood by and kept the coats they took off when they stoned him.’
NLT.21: “But the Lord said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles!’”
NLT.22: The crowd listened until Paul said that word. Then they all began to shout, “Away with such a fellow! He isn’t fit to live!”
NLT.23: They yelled, threw off their coats, and tossed handfuls of dust into the air.
NLT.24: The commander brought Paul inside and ordered him lashed with whips to make him confess his crime. He wanted to find out why the crowd had become so furious.
NLT.25: When they tied Paul down to lash him, Paul said to the officer standing there, “Is it legal for you to whip a Roman citizen who hasn’t even been tried?”
NLT.26: When the officer heard this, he went to the commander and asked, “What are you doing? This man is a Roman citizen!”
NLT.27: So the commander went over and asked Paul, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes, I certainly am,” Paul replied.
NLT.28: “I am, too,” the commander muttered, “and it cost me plenty!” Paul answered, “But I am a citizen by birth!”
NLT.29: The soldiers who were about to interrogate Paul quickly withdrew when they heard he was a Roman citizen, and the commander was frightened because he had ordered him bound and whipped.
NLT.30: The next day the commander ordered the leading priests into session with the Jewish high council. He wanted to find out what the trouble was all about, so he released Paul to have him stand before them.

WEB.1: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense which I now make to you.”
WEB.2: When they heard that he spoke to them in the Hebrew language, they were even more quiet. He said,
WEB.3: “I am indeed a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, instructed according to the strict tradition of the law of our fathers, being zealous for God, even as you all are today.
WEB.4: I persecuted this Way to the death, binding and delivering into prisons both men and women.
WEB.5: As also the high priest and all the council of the elders testify, from whom also I received letters to the brothers, and traveled to Damascus to bring them also who were there to Jerusalem in bonds to be punished.
WEB.6: As I made my journey, and came close to Damascus, about noon, suddenly a great light shone around me from the sky.
WEB.7: I fell to the ground, and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
WEB.8: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you persecute.’
WEB.9: “Those who were with me indeed saw the light and were afraid, but they didn’t understand the voice of him who spoke to me.
WEB.10: I said, ‘What shall I do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Arise, and go into Damascus. There you will be told about all things which are appointed for you to do.’
WEB.11: When I couldn’t see for the glory of that light, being led by the hand of those who were with me, I came into Damascus.
WEB.12: One Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well reported of by all the Jews who lived in Damascus,
WEB.13: came to me, and standing by me said to me, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ In that very hour I looked up at him.
WEB.14: He said, ‘The God of our fathers has appointed you to know his will, and to see the Righteous One, and to hear a voice from his mouth.
WEB.15: For you will be a witness for him to all men of what you have seen and heard.
WEB.16: Now why do you wait? Arise, be baptized, and wash away your sins, calling on the name of the Lord.’
WEB.17: “When I had returned to Jerusalem, and while I prayed in the temple, I fell into a trance,
WEB.18: and saw him saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not receive testimony concerning me from you.’
WEB.19: I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat in every synagogue those who believed in you.
WEB.20: When the blood of Stephen, your witness, was shed, I also was standing by, and consenting to his death, and guarding the cloaks of those who killed him.’
WEB.21: “He said to me, ‘Depart, for I will send you out far from here to the Gentiles.’”
WEB.22: They listened to him until he said that; then they lifted up their voice, and said, “Rid the earth of this fellow, for he isn’t fit to live!”
WEB.23: As they cried out, and threw off their cloaks, and threw dust into the air,
WEB.24: the commanding officer commanded him to be brought into the barracks, ordering him to be examined by scourging, that he might know for what crime they shouted against him like that.
WEB.25: When they had tied him up with thongs, Paul asked the centurion who stood by, “Is it lawful for you to scourge a man who is a Roman, and not found guilty?”
WEB.26: When the centurion heard it, he went to the commanding officer and told him, “Watch what you are about to do, for this man is a Roman!”
WEB.27: The commanding officer came and asked him, “Tell me, are you a Roman?” He said, “Yes.”
WEB.28: The commanding officer answered, “I bought my citizenship for a great price.” Paul said, “But I was born a Roman.”
WEB.29: Immediately those who were about to examine him departed from him, and the commanding officer also was afraid when he realized that he was a Roman, because he had bound him.
WEB.30: But on the next day, desiring to know the truth about why he was accused by the Jews, he freed him from the bonds, and commanded the chief priests and all the council to come together, and brought Paul down and set him before them.

CSB17.1: “Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense before you.”
CSB17.2: When they heard that he was addressing them in Aramaic, <sup>[1]</sup> they became even quieter. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.3: He <sup>ⓝ</sup> continued, “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia but brought up in this city, educated at the feet of Gamaliel <sup>ⓐ</sup> according to the law of our ancestors. I was zealous for God, just as all of you are today. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.4: I persecuted this Way <sup>ⓒ</sup> to the death, arresting and putting both men and women in jail, <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.5: as both the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. After I received letters from them to the brothers, I traveled to Damascus to arrest those who were there and bring them to Jerusalem to be punished. <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.6: “As <sup>ⓕ</sup> I was traveling and approaching Damascus, about noon an intense light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
CSB17.7: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
CSB17.8: “I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ “He said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, the one you are persecuting.’
CSB17.9: Now those who were with me saw the light, <sup>[2]</sup> but they did not hear the voice of the one who was speaking to me. <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.10: “I said, ‘What should I do, Lord?’ “The Lord told me, ‘Get up and go into Damascus, and there you will be told everything that you have been assigned to do.’
CSB17.11: “Since I couldn’t see because of the brightness of the light, <sup>[3]</sup> I was led by the hand by those who were with me, and went into Damascus. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.12: Someone named Ananias, a devout man according to the law, who had a good reputation with all the Jews living there, <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.13: came and stood by me and said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight.’ And in that very hour I looked up and saw him.
CSB17.14: And he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has appointed <sup>ⓙ</sup> you to know his will, to see <sup>ⓚ</sup> the Righteous One, <sup>ⓛ</sup> and to hear the words from his mouth,
CSB17.15: since you will be a witness for him to all people of what you have seen and heard. <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.16: And now, why are you delaying? Get up and be baptized, and wash away your sins, <sup>ⓝ</sup> calling on his name.’ <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.17: “After I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.18: and saw him telling me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
CSB17.19: “But I said, ‘Lord, they know that in synagogue after synagogue I had those who believed in you imprisoned and beaten. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.20: And when the blood of your witness Stephen was being shed, I stood there giving approval <sup>[4]</sup> and guarding the clothes of those who killed him.’ <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.21: “He said to me, ‘Go, because I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’  ” <sup>ⓔ</sup>
CSB17.22: They listened to him up to this point. Then they raised their voices, shouting, “Wipe this man off the face of the earth! He should not be allowed to live!” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.23: As they were yelling and flinging aside their garments and throwing dust into the air, <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.24: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, directing that he be interrogated with the scourge to discover the reason they were shouting against him like this.
CSB17.25: As they stretched him out for the lash, Paul said to the centurion standing by, “Is it legal for you to scourge a man who is a Roman citizen and is uncondemned?” <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.26: When the centurion heard this, he went and reported to the commander, saying, “What are you going to do? For this man is a Roman citizen.”
CSB17.27: The commander came and said to him, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” “Yes,” he said.
CSB17.28: The commander replied, “I bought this citizenship for a large amount of money.” “But I was born a citizen,” Paul said.
CSB17.29: So those who were about to examine him withdrew from him immediately. The commander too was alarmed when he realized Paul was a Roman citizen and he had bound him. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.30: The next day, since he wanted to find out exactly why Paul was being accused by the Jews, he released him <sup>[5]</sup> and instructed the chief priests and all the Sanhedrin to convene. <sup>ⓙ</sup> He brought Paul down and placed him before them.

AMP.1: "Brethren and fathers (kinsmen), hear my defense which I now offer to you."
AMP.2: When they heard that he was addressing them in the Hebrew dialect, they became even more quiet. And he continued,
AMP.3: "I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated at the feet of Gamaliel according to the strictness of the law of our fathers, being ardent and passionate for God just as all of you are today.
AMP.4: I persecuted and pursued the followers of this Way to the death, binding them with chains and putting [followers of Jesus] both men and women into prisons,
AMP.5: as the high priest and all the Council of the elders (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) can testify; because from them I received letters to the brothers, and I was on my way to Damascus in order to bring those [believers] who were there to Jerusalem in chains to be punished.
AMP.6: "But as I was on my way, approaching Damascus about noontime, a great blaze of light suddenly flashed from heaven and shone around me.
AMP.7: And I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, 'Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting Me?'
AMP.8: And I replied, 'Who are You, Lord?' And He said to me, 'I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are persecuting.'
AMP.9: Now those who were with me saw the light, but did not understand the voice of the One who was speaking to me.
AMP.10: And I asked, 'What shall I do, Lord?' And the Lord answered me, 'Get up and go into Damascus. There you will be told all that is appointed and destined for you to do.'
AMP.11: But since I could not see because of the [glorious intensity and dazzling] brightness of that light, I was led by the hand by those who were with me and came into Damascus.
AMP.12: "And one Ananias, a devout man according to the standard of the Law, and well spoken of by all the Jews who lived there,
AMP.13: came to [see] me, and standing near, he said to me, 'Brother Saul, receive your sight!' And at that very moment I [recovered my sight and] looked up at him.
AMP.14: And he said, 'The God of our fathers has appointed you to know His will, [and to progressively understand His plan with clarity and power] and to see the Righteous One [Jesus Christ, the Messiah] and to hear a message from His [own] mouth.
AMP.15: For you will be His witness to all men testifying of what you have seen and heard.
AMP.16: Now, why do you delay? Get up and be baptized, and wash away your sins by calling on His name [for salvation].'
AMP.17: "Then it happened when I had returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple [enclosure], that I fell into a trance (vision);
AMP.18: and I saw Him saying to me, 'Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about Me.'
AMP.19: And I said, 'Lord, they themselves know [without any doubt] that in one synagogue after another I used to imprison and beat those who believed in You [and Your message of salvation].
AMP.20: And when the blood of Your witness Stephen was being shed, I also was standing nearby approving and consenting [to his death], and guarding the coats of those who were killing him.'
AMP.21: And the Lord said to me, 'Go, I will send you far away to the Gentiles.'"
AMP.22: They listened to Paul until [he made] this [last] statement, but now they raised their voices and shouted, "Away with such a man from the earth! He is not fit to live!"
AMP.23: And as they were shouting and throwing off their coats [getting ready to stone Paul] and tossing dust into the air [expressing their anger],
AMP.24: the commander ordered him to be brought into the barracks, stating that he was to be interrogated with a whip in order to learn why the people were shouting against him that way.
AMP.25: But when they had stretched him out with the leather straps [in preparation for the whip], Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, "Is it legal for you to whip a man who is a Roman citizen and uncondemned [without a trial]?"
AMP.26: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and said to him, "What are you about to do? This man is a Roman!"
AMP.27: So the commander came and asked Paul, "Tell me, are you a Roman?" And he said, "Yes."
AMP.28: The commander replied, "I purchased this citizenship [of mine] for a large sum of money [so how did you acquire yours?]." Paul said, "But I was actually born a citizen."
AMP.29: So those who were about to interrogate him by torture immediately let him go; and the commander was also afraid when he realized that Paul was a Roman and he had put him in chains.
AMP.30: But on the next day, wanting to know the real reason why Paul was being accused by the Jews, he released him and ordered the chief priests and the whole Council (Sanhedrin, Jewish High Court) to assemble; and brought Paul down and presented him before them.

MSG.1: "My dear brothers and fathers, listen carefully to what I have to say before you jump to conclusions about me."
MSG.2: When they heard him speaking Hebrew, they grew even quieter. No one wanted to miss a word of this. He continued,
MSG.3: "I am a good Jew, born in Tarsus in the province of Cilicia, but educated here in Jerusalem under the exacting eye of Rabbi Gamaliel, thoroughly instructed in our religious traditions. And I've always been passionately on God's side, just as you are right now.
MSG.4: "I went after anyone connected with this 'Way,' went at them hammer and tongs, ready to kill for God. I rounded up men and women right and left and had them thrown in prison.
MSG.5: You can ask the Chief Priest or anyone in the High Council to verify this; they all knew me well. Then I went off to our brothers in Damascus, armed with official documents authorizing me to hunt down the Christians there, arrest them, and bring them back to Jerusalem for sentencing.
MSG.6: "As I arrived on the outskirts of Damascus about noon, a blinding light blazed out of the skies
MSG.7: and I fell to the ground, dazed. I heard a voice: 'Saul, Saul, why are you out to get me?'
MSG.8: " 'Who are you, Master?' I asked. "He said, 'I am Jesus the Nazarene, the One you're hunting down.'
MSG.9: My companions saw the light, but they didn't hear the conversation.
MSG.10: "Then I said, 'What do I do now, Master?' "He said, 'Get to your feet and enter Damascus. There you'll be told everything that's been set out for you to do.'
MSG.11: And so we entered Damascus, but nothing like the entrance I had planned—I was blind as a bat and my companions had to lead me in by the hand.
MSG.12: "And that's when I met Ananias, a man with a sterling reputation in observing our laws—the Jewish community in Damascus is unanimous on that score.
MSG.13: He came and put his arm on my shoulder. 'Look up,' he said. I looked, and found myself looking right into his eyes—I could see again!
MSG.14: "Then he said, 'The God of our ancestors has handpicked you to be briefed on his plan of action. You've actually seen the Righteous Innocent and heard him speak.
MSG.15: You are to be a key witness to everyone you meet of what you've seen and heard.
MSG.16: So what are you waiting for? Get up and get yourself baptized, scrubbed clean of those sins and personally acquainted with God.'
MSG.17: "Well, it happened just as Ananias said. After I was back in Jerusalem and praying one day in the Temple, lost in the presence of God,
MSG.18: I saw him, saw God's Righteous Innocent, and heard him say to me, 'Hurry up! Get out of here as quickly as you can. None of the Jews here in Jerusalem are going to accept what you say about me.'
MSG.19: "At first I objected: 'Who has better credentials? They all know how obsessed I was with hunting out those who believed in you, beating them up in the meeting places and throwing them in jail.
MSG.20: And when your witness Stephen was murdered, I was right there, holding the coats of the murderers and cheering them on. And now they see me totally converted. What better qualification could I have?'
MSG.21: "But he said, 'Don't argue. Go. I'm sending you on a long journey to outsider Gentiles.'"
MSG.22: The people in the crowd had listened attentively up to this point, but now they broke loose, shouting out, "Kill him! He's an insect! Stomp on him!"
MSG.23: They shook their fists. They filled the air with curses.
MSG.24: That's when the captain intervened and ordered Paul taken into the barracks. By now the captain was thoroughly exasperated. He decided to interrogate Paul under torture in order to get to the bottom of this, to find out what he had done that provoked this outraged violence.
MSG.25: As they spread-eagled him with thongs, getting him ready for the whip, Paul said to the centurion standing there, "Is this legal: torturing a Roman citizen without a fair trial?"
MSG.26: When the centurion heard that, he went directly to the captain. "Do you realize what you've done? This man is a Roman citizen!"
MSG.27: The captain came back and took charge. "Is what I hear right? You're a Roman citizen?" Paul said, "I certainly am."
MSG.28: The captain was impressed. "I paid a huge sum for my citizenship. How much did it cost you?" "Nothing," said Paul. "It cost me nothing. I was free from the day of my birth."
MSG.29: That put a stop to the interrogation. And it put the fear of God into the captain. He had put a Roman citizen in chains and come within a whisker of putting him under torture!
MSG.30: The next day, determined to get to the root of the trouble and know for sure what was behind the Jewish accusation, the captain released Paul and ordered a meeting of the high priests and the High Council to see what they could make of it. Paul was led in and took his place before them.

NIV.1: 22 “Brothers and fathers, listen now to my defense.”
NIV.2:
When they heard him speak to them in Aramaic, they became very quiet.
Then Paul said:
NIV.3: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus of Cilicia, but brought up in this city. Under Gamaliel I was thoroughly trained in the law of our fathers and was just as zealous for God as any of you are today.
NIV.4: I persecuted the followers of this Way to their death, arresting both men and women and throwing them into prison,
NIV.5: as also the high priest and all the Council can testify. I even obtained letters from them to their brothers in Damascus, and went there to bring these people as prisoners to Jerusalem to be punished.
NIV.6: “About noon as I came near Damascus, suddenly a bright light from heaven flashed around me.
NIV.7: I fell to the ground and heard a voice say to me, ‘Saul! Saul! Why do you persecute me?’
NIV.8:
“‘Who are you, Lord?’ I asked.
“ ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth, whom you are persecuting,’ he replied.
NIV.9: My companions saw the light, but they did not understand the voice of him who was speaking to me.
NIV.10:
“‘What shall I do, Lord?’ I asked.
“ ‘Get up,’ the Lord said, ‘and go into Damascus. There you will be told all that you have been assigned to do.’
NIV.11: My companions led me by the hand into Damascus, because the brilliance of the light had blinded me.
NIV.12: “A man named Ananias came to see me. He was a devout observer of the law and highly respected by all the Jews living there.
NIV.13: He stood beside me and said, ‘Brother Saul, receive your sight!’ And at that very moment I was able to see him.
NIV.14: “Then he said: ‘The God of our fathers has chosen you to know his will and to see the Righteous One and to hear words from his mouth.
NIV.15: You will be his witness to all men of what you have seen and heard.
NIV.16: And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized and wash your sins away, calling on his name.’
NIV.17: “When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying at the temple, I fell into a trance
NIV.18: and saw the Lord speaking. ‘Quick!’ he said to me. ‘Leave Jerusalem immediately, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
NIV.19: “‘Lord,’ I replied, ‘these men know that I went from one synagogue to another to imprison and beat those who believe in you.
NIV.20: And when the blood of your martyr Stephen was shed, I stood there giving my approval and guarding the clothes of those who were killing him.’
NIV.21: “Then the Lord said to me, ‘Go; I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’ ”
NIV.22:
Paul the Roman Citizen
The crowd listened to Paul until he said this. Then they raised their voices and shouted, “Rid the earth of him! He’s not fit to live!”
NIV.23: As they were shouting and throwing off their cloaks and flinging dust into the air,
NIV.24: the commander ordered Paul to be taken into the barracks. He directed that he be flogged and questioned in order to find out why the people were shouting at him like this.
NIV.25: As they stretched him out to flog him, Paul said to the centurion standing there, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who hasn’t even been found guilty?”
NIV.26: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commander and reported it. “What are you going to do?” he asked. “This man is a Roman citizen.”
NIV.27:
The commander went to Paul and asked, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?”
“Yes, I am,” he answered.
NIV.28:
Then the commander said, “I had to pay a big price for my citizenship.”
“But I was born a citizen,” Paul replied.
NIV.29: Those who were about to question him withdrew immediately. The commander himself was alarmed when he realized that he had put Paul, a Roman citizen, in chains.
NIV.30:
Before the Sanhedrin
The next day, since the commander wanted to find out exactly why Paul was being accused by the Jews, he released him and ordered the chief priests and all the Sanhedrin to assemble. Then he brought Paul and had him stand before them.

NRSVCE.1: “Brothers and fathers, listen to the defense that I now make before you.”
NRSVCE.2: When they heard him addressing them in Hebrew, they became even more quiet. Then he said:
NRSVCE.3: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city at the feet of Gamaliel, educated strictly according to our ancestral law, being zealous for God, just as all of you are today.
NRSVCE.4: I persecuted this Way up to the point of death by binding both men and women and putting them in prison,
NRSVCE.5: as the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. From them I also received letters to the brothers in Damascus, and I went there in order to bind those who were there and to bring them back to Jerusalem for punishment.
NRSVCE.6: “While I was on my way and approaching Damascus, about noon a great light from heaven suddenly shone about me.
NRSVCE.7: I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
NRSVCE.8: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ Then he said to me, ‘I am Jesus of Nazareth whom you are persecuting.’
NRSVCE.9: Now those who were with me saw the light but did not hear the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
NRSVCE.10: I asked, ‘What am I to do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go to Damascus; there you will be told everything that has been assigned to you to do.’
NRSVCE.11: Since I could not see because of the brightness of that light, those who were with me took my hand and led me to Damascus.
NRSVCE.12: “A certain Ananias, who was a devout man according to the law and well spoken of by all the Jews living there,
NRSVCE.13: came to me; and standing beside me, he said, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight!’ In that very hour I regained my sight and saw him.
NRSVCE.14: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has chosen you to know his will, to see the Righteous One and to hear his own voice;
NRSVCE.15: for you will be his witness to all the world of what you have seen and heard.
NRSVCE.16: And now why do you delay? Get up, be baptized, and have your sins washed away, calling on his name.’
NRSVCE.17: “After I had returned to Jerusalem and while I was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
NRSVCE.18: and saw Jesus saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
NRSVCE.19: And I said, ‘Lord, they themselves know that in every synagogue I imprisoned and beat those who believed in you.
NRSVCE.20: And while the blood of your witness Stephen was shed, I myself was standing by, approving and keeping the coats of those who killed him.’
NRSVCE.21: Then he said to me, ‘Go, for I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
NRSVCE.22: Up to this point they listened to him, but then they shouted, “Away with such a fellow from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live.”
NRSVCE.23: And while they were shouting, throwing off their cloaks, and tossing dust into the air,
NRSVCE.24: the tribune directed that he was to be brought into the barracks, and ordered him to be examined by flogging, to find out the reason for this outcry against him.
NRSVCE.25: But when they had tied him up with thongs, Paul said to the centurion who was standing by, “Is it legal for you to flog a Roman citizen who is uncondemned?”
NRSVCE.26: When the centurion heard that, he went to the tribune and said to him, “What are you about to do? This man is a Roman citizen.”
NRSVCE.27: The tribune came and asked Paul, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” And he said, “Yes.”
NRSVCE.28: The tribune answered, “It cost me a large sum of money to get my citizenship.” Paul said, “But I was born a citizen.”
NRSVCE.29: Immediately those who were about to examine him drew back from him; and the tribune also was afraid, for he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had bound him.
NRSVCE.30: Since he wanted to find out what Paul was being accused of by the Jews, the next day he released him and ordered the chief priests and the entire council to meet. He brought Paul down and had him stand before them.

NET.1: “Brothers and fathers, listen to my defense that I now make to you.”
NET.2: (When they heard that he was addressing them in Aramaic, they became even quieter.) Then Paul said,
NET.3: “I am a Jew, born in Tarsus in Cilicia, but brought up in this city, educated with strictness under Gamaliel according to the law of our ancestors, and was zealous for God just as all of you are today.
NET.4: I persecuted this Way even to the point of death, tying up both men and women and putting them in prison,
NET.5: as both the high priest and the whole council of elders can testify about me. From them I also received letters to the brothers in Damascus, and I was on my way to make arrests there and bring the prisoners to Jerusalem to be punished.
NET.6: As I was en route and near Damascus, about noon a very bright light from heaven suddenly flashed around me.
NET.7: Then I fell to the ground and heard a voice saying to me, ‘Saul, Saul, why are you persecuting me?’
NET.8: I answered, ‘Who are you, Lord?’ He said to me, ‘I am Jesus the Nazarene, whom you are persecuting.’
NET.9: Those who were with me saw the light, but did not understand the voice of the one who was speaking to me.
NET.10: So I asked, ‘What should I do, Lord?’ The Lord said to me, ‘Get up and go to Damascus; there you will be told about everything that you have been designated to do.’
NET.11: Since I could not see because of the brilliance of that light, I came to Damascus led by the hand of those who were with me.
NET.12: A man named Ananias, a devout man according to the law, well spoken of by all the Jews who live there,
NET.13: came to me and stood beside me and said to me, ‘Brother Saul, regain your sight!’ And at that very moment I looked up and saw him.
NET.14: Then he said, ‘The God of our ancestors has already chosen you to know his will, to see the Righteous One, and to hear a command from his mouth,
NET.15: because you will be his witness to all people of what you have seen and heard.
NET.16: And now what are you waiting for? Get up, be baptized, and have your sins washed away, calling on his name.’
NET.17: When I returned to Jerusalem and was praying in the temple, I fell into a trance
NET.18: and saw the Lord saying to me, ‘Hurry and get out of Jerusalem quickly, because they will not accept your testimony about me.’
NET.19: I replied, ‘Lord, they themselves know that I imprisoned and beat those in the various synagogues who believed in you.
NET.20: And when the blood of your witness Stephen was shed, I myself was standing nearby, approving, and guarding the cloaks of those who were killing him.’
NET.21: Then he said to me, ‘Go, because I will send you far away to the Gentiles.’”
NET.22: The crowd was listening to him until he said this. Then they raised their voices and shouted, “Away with this man from the earth! For he should not be allowed to live!”
NET.23: While they were screaming and throwing off their cloaks and tossing dust in the air,
NET.24: the commanding officer ordered Paul to be brought back into the barracks. He told them to interrogate Paul by beating him with a lash so that he could find out the reason the crowd was shouting at Paul in this way.
NET.25: When they had stretched him out for the lash, Paul said to the centurion standing nearby, “Is it legal for you to lash a man who is a Roman citizen without a proper trial?”
NET.26: When the centurion heard this, he went to the commanding officer and reported it, saying, “What are you about to do? For this man is a Roman citizen.”
NET.27: So the commanding officer came and asked Paul, “Tell me, are you a Roman citizen?” He replied, “Yes.”
NET.28: The commanding officer answered, “I acquired this citizenship with a large sum of money.” “But I was even born a citizen,” Paul replied.
NET.29: Then those who were about to interrogate him stayed away from him, and the commanding officer was frightened when he realized that Paul was a Roman citizen and that he had had him tied up.
NET.30: The next day, because the commanding officer wanted to know the true reason Paul was being accused by the Jews, he released him and ordered the chief priests and the whole council to assemble. He then brought Paul down and had him stand before them.
